# SPEC: #212 — refactor: rename 'recipes' to 'plays' across the entire codebase
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Rename every occurrence of 'recipe'/'recipes' to 'play'/'plays' across the Meridian OS codebase — exhaustively, with no references remaining.
**Constraints:** Exhaustive coverage; preserve semantics; handle all case variants (recipe, Recipe, RECIPE, recipes, Recipes); update both directory names and content; config key and path consistency maintained throughout; the L1/L2 level qualifier must be dropped when combined with "recipe" (e.g., "L1 recipe" becomes "play", not "atomic play").
**Failure Conditions:** Any remaining 'recipe'/'recipes' string (case-insensitive) in any git-tracked file after completion; broken path references; config/code key mismatch between `components.plays:` key and scripts referencing `recipes/` directory; `{recipe-name}` template variable not updated to `{play-name}` in SKILL.md files.

## Summary

This refactor converts Meridian OS from a cooking metaphor to a sports metaphor. The rename touches five distinct layers: (1) the canonical source-of-truth directory `core/components/recipes/` and its 18 play subdirectories, (2) documentation files including an ADR named after "recipe maturity model," a framework file named "recipe-structure.md," and a component guide named "recipes.md," (3) configuration and script files — `core/config.yaml` (one config key, one comment, two template variable strings), `installer/install.sh`, `sync-claude/scripts/sync.sh`, and `sync-droids/scripts/sync.sh`, (4) inline references inside all agent `.md` files, all other SKILL.md files, memory standards, ADRs, and philosophy docs, and (5) git-tracked STM/project history under `.meridian/` and `.claude/specs/`.

The approach must sequence directory renames (`git mv`) before content edits to preserve git history. The L1/L2-to-play conversion is semantically distinct from the mechanical find-replace: every occurrence of "L1 recipe," "L2 recipe," "L1 Recipes," "L2 Recipes," and the three-layer hierarchy description must be updated with deliberate rewording. The `{recipe-name}` template variable in SKILL.md files, and `recipe_name` shell variable names in sync scripts, must also be updated. After all source renames and content edits are complete, regenerating `~/.claude/`, `~/.factory/`, and `~/.meridian/` via `/sync-claude` and `/sync-droids` handles the gitignored deployed copies automatically.

## Technical Approach

**Strategy:** Three-pass execution in strict dependency order — (1) directory and file renames via `git mv` first to establish new paths, (2) mechanical content replacement via scripted sed across all renamed files and files with embedded references, (3) deliberate manual rewording for L1/L2 level-dropping cases and section titles that require semantic judgment. Scripts and config keys are content edits in pass 2 but receive focused attention as the primary integration points. After all source changes land, run `/sync-claude` and `/sync-droids` to regenerate the gitignored deployed copies.

**Alternatives Considered:**
- Content-first then renames → Rejected: `git mv` on directories after content edits risks git treating the operation as delete+add, losing history on individual files within the directory.
- Single sed pass with regex → Partially viable for mechanical cases but inadequate for L1/L2 level-dropping (a regex replacing "recipe" with "play" would produce "atomic play" which is wrong). Requires a separate deliberate pass.
- Treat `.meridian/` and `.claude/` STM history as out-of-scope → Rejected: these directories are git-tracked; "no nook or cranny missed" applies.

**Risks:**

| Risk | Mitigation | Severity |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `{recipe-name}` variable not fully replaced in all SKILL.md interpolation points | Grep for `{recipe-name}` and `recipe_name` across all SKILL.md and `.sh` files as pre-pass | High |
| L1/L2 level-dropping produces invalid strings like "atomic play" | Separate T9 task with explicit search-and-replace list; human review before commit | High |
| Path breakage: config key not updated atomically with directory rename | T6 updates config.yaml immediately after T1 directory rename | High |
| `installer/install.sh` still references `$COMPONENTS_DIR/recipes` after source rename | T7 covers installer and both sync scripts explicitly | High |
| 16 separate `.meridian/` create-recipe directory renames | Use a shell loop across all 16 issue numbers | Medium |
| `recipe-analysis.md` files need per-file renames | Use `git ls-files` pipe after directory renames | Medium |
| `.claude/settings.local.json` is gitignored but has a hardcoded path | Document as a manual note; not a tracked file failure condition | Low |
